title: NLS focusing bright soliton — Madelung-Psi split-step validation
ic: Psi(x,0) = sqrt(2) * sech(sqrt(2)*(x+5)) * exp(i*0.25*x). (Bright soliton, amplitude sqrt(2), at x=-5, moving at speed v/2 = 0.25.)
ground_truth: Exact sech bright soliton, mass(N) = 2*sqrt(2) exactly conserved, peak amplitude = 2 exactly conserved, peak position translates at speed 0.25, no shape change.
stress_question: What is the minimum dt for split-step Fourier accuracy? At what Nx does the method fail? Does it conserve mass to machine precision?
extra_method_to_try: After split-step Fourier works, try direct (N, phi) evolution via FFT (no Psi reconstruction) to compare; report relative accuracy.
